app.title: Cluster Installer
app.subtitle: Unattended openSUSE / MicroOS + Ceph + RKE2/K3s install

1.title: Select project
1.subtitle: Start a new cluster or resume an in-progress run

2.title: Choose target environment
2.subtitle: Where the VMs (or bare metal) will be hosted

3.title: OS image
3.subtitle: Different roles can use different operating systems

6.title: Install
6.subtitle: Unattended OS install → Ceph → K8s → CSI → addons

step1.modeNewDesc: Start from a blank inventory (Ceph / K8s / combined)
step1.modeNewVMDesc: Single-VM unattended install verification — ESXi + Ubuntu. The cluster's smallest unit.
step1.devVMSubtitle: Build one VM and verify the OS install end-to-end — the foundation cluster-mode OS install relies on.
step1.devVMNote: Next steps: enter ESXi credentials, pick Ubuntu version, then hostname/IP/SSH keys.
step1.topologyHint: Ceph and Kubernetes have independent lifecycles. Building them separately makes failures easier to debug and re-run.
step1.topologyCephDesc: Distributed block / file / object storage only. mon×3 + mgr + osd×N (+ optional rgw). Outputs: Ceph dashboard, RBD/CephFS, S3 endpoint. Other K8s clusters or VMs can connect via the client.k8s keyring.
step1.topologyK8sDesc: Container workloads only. control-plane × {1 or 3} + worker × N. Outputs: kubeconfig, Ingress, GitOps. Storage: external Ceph (existing) / local disk / no PVC.
step1.topologyCombinedDesc: ⚠ Advanced — Ceph first → K8s → CSI bridge, all automatic. 60+ min runtime, failure debugging needs you to know which stage broke. Recommended for fresh greenfield only.
step1.contentRepoHint: GitHub repo holding Terraform modules + Ansible playbooks
step1.noRuns: No saved runs. Start a new cluster on the left.

step2.libvirtDesc: Connect over SSH to a Linux KVM host. Fastest validation path.
step2.proxmoxDesc: Use the Proxmox API. Common in Korean SMB deployments.
step2.esxiDesc: Drive an ESXi host via vSphere SOAP/REST. ISO uploads via datastore HTTP. SSH used as a fallback for esxcli + direct datastore access.
step2.sshKeyHintEsxi: Optional — if blank, the root password is reused for SSH.
step2.rootPassword: Root password (shared by vSphere API + SSH)
step2.rootPasswordHint: ESXi uses a single root password for both vSphere SOAP and SSH. Stored in %LOCALAPPDATA%\cluster-installer\runs\<id>\run.json — do not export.
step2.datastoreHint: Name of an ESXi storage location — VM virtual disks (.vmdk) and seed ISOs go there. e.g. datastore1, SSD-RAID0-4Ti-02
step2.isoDatastoreHint: Where install/seed ISOs are uploaded. VM virtual disks are picked per-node in Step 4 — that separation makes the "small datastore catches every OSD disk" footgun structurally impossible.
step2.networkHint: VM-facing port group on the ESXi vSwitch. ESXi default: 'VM Network'.
step2.datastoreHelpTitle: How do I find my datastore / port group names?
step2.datastoreHelpBody: ESXi groups its disks/arrays under names called 'datastores'. Don't guess — use one of:
step2.datastoreHelpStep1: Click 'Connect + discover resources' above (easiest, auto-fills the dropdown)
step2.datastoreHelpStep2: ESXi web UI: https://<ESXi IP>/ → left sidebar 'Storage' → 'Datastores' tab
step2.datastoreHelpStep3: Or via SSH: esxcli storage filesystem list
step2.tlsInsecure: Skip TLS certificate verification (self-signed labs only)
step2.advertiseIPHint: Windows host IP that target VMs will reach for Agama profiles. Leave blank to auto-pick.

step3.perRoleHint: Different roles can use different OSs. Typically Ceph nodes run Leap, K8s nodes run MicroOS.
step3.esxiOnlyMicroOS: ESXi target only supports MicroOS for unattended install in this release — Leap/Tumbleweed need Agama ISO remaster (phase-1 §4), tracked for the next milestone. Apply will fail at the datastore_upload stage with a clear error if you keep this combination.

image.microosDesc: Immutable container host. Auto-updates, transactional-update. Recommended for K8s workloads.
image.leapDesc: Stable LTS, immediate zypper installs. Recommended for Ceph OSD/MON nodes that need package flexibility.
image.ubuntuDesc: Canonical LTS Server. Subiquity autoinstall (cloud-init NoCloud) for unattended install. Officially supported by Ceph cephadm, RKE2, and K3s. apt + AppArmor environment.
image.ubuntu26Desc: Primary automation target. cloud-init + subiquity autoinstall, full apt ecosystem, vmxnet3 (ens192) out of the box.
image.ubuntu24Desc: Regression safety net. If 26.04 subiquity schema breaks autoinstall, 24.04 is the fastest comparison.

step3.devVMHint: Build one independent VM with unattended OS install. Just pick the OS.
step3.devVMOnlyUbuntuNote: v1: Ubuntu LTS only. Other distros (Leap, RHEL family, etc.) extend the same flow once added to the catalog — coming later.

step4.vipHint: API server VIP advertised by kube-vip (ARP)

node.clusterIPDisabledHint: This node has no OSD role; cluster network IP is not used.
node.osReadOnlyHint: Auto-set from Step 3 (per role: K8s vs Ceph). To change, go back to Step 3.

step4.devVMSubtitle: Build one independent VM and verify the unattended OS install end-to-end. Cluster, CIDR, role settings are not used.
step4.devVMSectionHint: Hostname, IP, and resource sizing — that's all. SSH keys come from what you entered on Step 1.

devVM.ipModeStaticHint: Install with the IP/gateway you specify. Most common.
devVM.ipModeDHCPHint: DHCP server assigns at boot. No IP/gateway input needed.
devVM.ipDHCPHint: (optional) for SSH during verify — fill in if you have a DHCP reservation
devVM.dhcpNote: DHCP mode: static IP / gateway inputs are ignored. autoinstall sets the NIC to dhcp4: true. The verify stage needs a known IP to SSH into; provide one only if you have a DHCP reservation.
devVM.datastoreHint: ESXi datastore that hosts the VM's virtual disks. Click 'Discover resources' on Step 2 to populate the dropdown.
devVM.diskProvisioningHint: thin: allocate as used (default). thick: pre-allocate. thick-eager: pre-allocate + zero (most consistent I/O).
devVM.hostnameHint: Goes into /etc/hostname, the shell prompt, the SSH host-key identifier, etc.
devVM.displayNameHint: Label shown in the vSphere inventory tree. Empty = reuse the hostname above. Set this when you want different values inside vs outside the VM.
devVM.nameserverHint: Each entry must be a dotted-quad IP (e.g. 10.10.1.3 — a typo like '10.10.13' with a missing octet is rejected by netplan apply)
devVM.note: This mode ignores cluster.kubernetes / network.vip / lb_pool / Ceph entirely — only the single-VM unattended install runs.

step5.consent: Confirms creation. VMs will be created and OSes freshly installed.

step6.serverURLHint: VMs fetch Agama profiles from this URL
step6.firewallHint: Windows Firewall may show an Allow dialog on first inbound — click 'Allow access'.
step6.cancelConfirm: Abort the current stage and mark the run as failed. Already-created VMs are left in place (delete them separately if needed). Continue?
step6.verifySubtitle: SSH into the VM and run four sanity checks.

nextStepsList: Run ceph -s on a mon node to verify storage health
Push a first Argo CD Application if you opted into GitOps
step7.devVMVerifyHint: PASS/FAIL record from the most recent verify stage.
step7.devVMRedeployHint: Re-create the same VM with the same inventory (re-run terraform apply). MAC and IP are kept stable.
step7.devVMRedeployNote: v1: delete the VM in vSphere first, then redeploy. Full destroy+apply automation is planned for the next release.
